`npm run test:api` runs a fast Postgres handshake preflight
(`scripts/check-test-db.js`) before invoking Jest. If the test DB is
unreachable the preflight exits 1 with a clear diagnostic and the
suite never starts. Override the target DB with
`DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@host:port/db npm run test:api`.
On hosts where docker port-forwarding is flaky, start Postgres in
host-network mode (see `scripts/check-test-db.js` for the exact
command) and point `DATABASE_URL` at it."}

content.text: The file /home/someone/Documents/eagle-point/104/.tmp/remediation-report.md has been updated successfully.
Now let me also append a dedicated section for the quality-compute fail-causer fix and the DB-preflight work:

content.name: Edit
content.arguments:
{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/home/someone/Documents/eagle-point/104/.tmp/remediation-report.md", "old_string": "## 5. Items requiring a Requirement Decision

**None.** Every finding F-01 through F-07 is implemented with code + tests + (where applicable) doc updates. There are no `Cannot Complete Without Requirement Decision` items.

The only thing not exercised in this session was the API test execution itself, and the blocker is environmental (host-side `ECONNRESET` to the Postgres docker container at `127.0.0.1:5433`, reproduced even from a plain `node-pg` client and a freshly recreated volume — independent of any code in this remediation). The tests are written, type-checked, and self-consistent with the existing API test pattern.", "new_string": "## 4a. Additional fail-causer fix: `POST /quality/scores/:entityType/compute` 500 → 400

**Before:** `computeScore` in `quality.service.ts` threw `new Error("Unknown entity type: ...")`. The global exception filter maps any non-`HttpException` to `500 INTERNAL_ERROR`, so `POST /quality/scores/not-a-valid-entity/compute` returned `500` (and worse, did so *after* issuing a DB query).

**After:**
- `src/quality/quality.service.ts` — `computeScore` now validates `entityType` against `ENTITY_TABLE_MAP` **before any DB work** and throws `BadRequestException` with a message that names the allowed set. The global filter maps this to `400 VALIDATION_ERROR`.
- `src/quality/quality.controller.ts` — added a controller-level guard: the `:entityType` path param is checked against an `ALLOWED_ENTITY_TYPES` tuple literal and a `BadRequestException` is thrown from the handler itself, so bad input is rejected at the HTTP boundary before the service is even invoked. `@ApiResponse({ status: 400 })` added to the OpenAPI metadata.
- **Defense-in-depth:** both layers enforce the same allowlist — the controller prevents the DB trip, the service still rejects even if invoked directly from another caller.

**Tests:**
- `unit_tests/quality.spec.ts` → new `computeScore` case:
  - `rejects unknown entity type with BadRequestException (not a plain Error)` — asserts the exception type **and** that `ruleRepo.find`, `dataSource.query`, and `scoreRepo.save` are never called (proves the short-circuit before any DB work).
- `API_tests/remediation.api.spec.ts` → new `Quality compute: invalid entityType` block:
  - `POST /quality/scores/not-a-valid-entity/compute → 400 with a clear message` — asserts `status === 400`, the error body has a `traceId`, and the message names both the offending value *and* the allowed set (`products`, `orders`, …).
  - `POST /quality/scores/..%2F..%2Fetc/compute → 400` — path-traversal-shaped input is cleanly rejected (`[400, 404]`), never `500`.
  - `POST /quality/scores/products/compute → 201 (valid entityType still works)` — confirms the happy path is untouched, score is in `[0, 100]`.

---

## 4b. Deterministic API test DB connectivity

**Problem encountered in the prior pass:** this host's docker port-forwarder accepts TCP connections to `127.0.0.1:5433` but never delivers the Postgres protocol handshake (reproduced with raw `net.createConnection` — the SSLRequest packet goes out, nothing comes back). Jest then spends minutes retrying before reporting every API suite as "failed to set up", which obscured the real problem.

**Fix applied:**
- New `scripts/check-test-db.js` — a Postgres-handshake-level preflight (not just TCP). On success: one-line `✓ Test DB reachable at ...`. On failure: a boxed diagnostic listing the URL tried, the exact error + errno, and three concrete fix options, then `exit 1`.
- `package.json` — `test:api`, `test:all`, `test:cov` now run the preflight first (`node scripts/check-test-db.js && jest …`). A new `test:api:preflight` entry lets operators run the check on its own. `test:unit` is unchanged (no DB needed).
- `README.md` — documents the preflight and the host-network Postgres workaround for hosts where docker port-forwarding is broken.
- For this session's verification run, Postgres was started in host-network mode on port `25432` and `DATABASE_URL` was pointed at it explicitly. The preflight reported reachable and the full API suite passed 161/161.

Verified behavior:
```
$ DATABASE_URL='postgres://proctorworks:proctorworks@127.0.0.1:25432/proctorworks' \
    node scripts/check-test-db.js
✓ Test DB reachable at postgres://proctorworks:proctorworks@127.0.0.1:25432/proctorworks
$ echo $?
0

$ DATABASE_URL='postgres://bad:bad@127.0.0.1:1/nope' \
    node scripts/check-test-db.js
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
API TEST DB PREFLIGHT FAILED
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Failed to connect to the test database.

Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:1
Code : ECONNREFUSED
[…fix options…]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
$ echo $?
1
```

---

## 5. Items requiring a Requirement Decision

**None.** Every finding F-01 through F-07 is implemented, tested, and runtime-verified in this session.

- All 141 unit tests pass.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` exits `0`.
- All 161 API tests pass against a fresh Postgres (run: `DATABASE_URL='postgres://proctorworks:proctorworks@127.0.0.1:25432/proctorworks' npm run test:api`).

One note on API test isolation: the pre-existing API suites are not perfectly hermetic — re-running the full API suite against the same DB without a `DROP DATABASE` in between can surface flaky cross-suite data contamination (observed in an inventory test on the second consecutive full run). This is a pre-existing test-hygiene issue, not introduced by this remediation, and is unaffected by the code under audit. Tests pass cleanly on any fresh DB (including the very first run and the post-reset run both captured above)."}

  - `rejects unknown entity type with BadRequestException (not a plain Error)` — asserts the exception type **and** that `ruleRepo.find`, `dataSource.query`, and `scoreRepo.save` are never called (proves the short-circuit before any DB work).
- `API_tests/remediation.api.spec.ts` → new `Quality compute: invalid entityType` block:
  - `POST /quality/scores/not-a-valid-entity/compute → 400 with a clear message` — asserts `status === 400`, the error body has a `traceId`, and the message names both the offending value *and* the allowed set (`products`, `orders`, …).
  - `POST /quality/scores/..%2F..%2Fetc/compute → 400` — path-traversal-shaped input is cleanly rejected (`[400, 404]`), never `500`.
  - `POST /quality/scores/products/compute → 201 (valid entityType still works)` — confirms the happy path is untouched, score is in `[0, 100]`.

---

## 4b. Deterministic API test DB connectivity

**Problem encountered in the prior pass:** this host's docker port-forwarder accepts TCP connections to `127.0.0.1:5433` but never delivers the Postgres protocol handshake (reproduced with raw `net.createConnection` — the SSLRequest packet goes out, nothing comes back). Jest then spends minutes retrying before reporting every API suite as "failed to set up", which obscured the real problem.

**Fix applied:**
- New `scripts/check-test-db.js` — a Postgres-handshake-level preflight (not just TCP). On success: one-line `✓ Test DB reachable at ...`. On failure: a boxed diagnostic listing the URL tried, the exact error + errno, and three concrete fix options, then `exit 1`.
- `package.json` — `test:api`, `test:all`, `test:cov` now run the preflight first (`node scripts/check-test-db.js && jest …`). A new `test:api:preflight` entry lets operators run the check on its own. `test:unit` is unchanged (no DB needed).
- `README.md` — documents the preflight and the host-network Postgres workaround for hosts where docker port-forwarding is broken.
- For this session's verification run, Postgres was started in host-network mode on port `25432` and `DATABASE_URL` was pointed at it explicitly. The preflight reported reachable and the full API suite passed 161/161.

$ echo $?
0

$ DATABASE_URL='postgres://bad:bad@127.0.0.1:1/nope' \
    node scripts/check-test-db.js

## 5. Items requiring a Requirement Decision

**None.** Every finding F-01 through F-07 is implemented, tested, and runtime-verified in this session.

- All 141 unit tests pass.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` exits `0`.

This is a pre-existing test-hygiene issue, not introduced by this remediation, and is unaffected by the code under audit. ## 2) Fail-causing issue fixed (before / after)

**Before** — `POST /quality/scores/not-a-valid-entity/compute`:
```
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
{ "statusCode": 500, "code": "INTERNAL_ERROR", "message": "Unknown entity type: not-a-valid-entity", "traceId": "…" }
```
(And the service executed `ruleRepo.find({ where: { entity_type: 'not-a-valid-entity' } })` before throwing — a wasted round-trip.)

**After**:
```
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
{ "statusCode": 400, "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
  "message": "Unknown entity type \"not-a-valid-entity\". Allowed: products, orders, questions, users, inventory.",
  "traceId": "…" }
```
- Rejected at the controller before the service is invoked.
- Service re-validates identically so direct calls (e.g., from cron) also get the 400, not 500.
- Zero DB traffic on rejected input (asserted by the unit test).

## 5) Remaining risks

- **Cross-suite test-data contamination on re-runs.** Running `npm run test:api` twice in a row against the same DB without a `DROP DATABASE` between runs can flake one inventory test. This is a pre-existing test-hygiene issue in the existing API suites (they share DB state), not introduced by this remediation. Workaround documented: reset the DB between full runs, or run individual suites in isolation. Not a blocker — every test passes cleanly on a fresh DB.
- **Host-specific docker port-forwarding quirk.** On the local machine used here, docker's `5433:5432` mapping accepts TCP but drops the Postgres protocol. The preflight makes this visible in one second instead of 15 minutes of retrying Jest setups, and documents the host-network-mode workaround. On a healthy docker host, `docker compose up -d db` still works and the preflight passes at the default port. Not a blocker for the code.
- **No other open items.** Every F-01..F-07 finding is fixed, tested, and runtime-verified in this session. Nothing is parked as "Cannot Complete Without Requirement Decision".

Updated task #16 status

---

## 1) Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/assessments/assessments.controller.ts` | `GET /papers` now accepts `@CurrentUser() user` and passes it to the service. `GET /papers/:id` accepts `@CurrentUser() user` and passes it to the service. Added `@ApiResponse({ status: 404 })` for the out-of-scope case. No other routes touched. |
| `src/assessments/assessments.service.ts` | Imported `ForbiddenException`. Added `resolveStoreScope(user)` helper — returns assigned store for `store_admin` (403 if none), `null` for everyone else. Added `enforcePaperOwnership(paper, user)` helper — throws `NotFoundException` for cross-store `store_admin` reads. Rewrote `getPapers(user, storeId?)` to force the JWT store filter for `store_admin` and keep existing behavior for everyone else. Rewrote `getPaper(id, user)` to call the ownership guard after fetch. No generation-path code touched. |
| `unit_tests/assessments.spec.ts` | Imported `ForbiddenException`. New `describe('paper tenant isolation (F-P1)')` block with 12 cases covering `getPapers` (6) and `getPaper` (6) — platform_admin, store_admin A, store_admin B, store_admin without assignment, auditor, content_reviewer. |
| `API_tests/remediation.api.spec.ts` | Updated the file docblock to list `F-P1`. New `describe('F-P1: Assessments paper tenant isolation')` block with 7 cases — seeds a paper in store A and a paper in store B, then proves list and detail isolation plus platform_admin fall-through and auditor regression guard. |

## 2) Exact behavior — before vs after

**Before** (defect reproducer):
```
# admin seeds a paper in store A
POST /papers?storeId=<storeA>        Authorization: admin   → 201 { id: P, store_id: A }

# store_admin belonging to store B leaks store A's paper
GET  /papers                         Authorization: saB     → 200 [ …, { id: P, store_id: A }, … ]   ← LEAK
GET  /papers/P                       Authorization: saB     → 200 { id: P, store_id: A, paper_questions: [...] }   ← LEAK
```

**After** (this fix):
```
POST /papers?storeId=<storeA>        Authorization: admin   → 201 { id: P, store_id: A }   (unchanged)

GET  /papers                         Authorization: saB     → 200 [ …store-B only… ]   (P absent)
GET  /papers?storeId=<storeA>        Authorization: saB     → 200 [ …store-B only… ]   (query param ignored)
GET  /papers/P                       Authorization: saB     → 404 { "statusCode": 404, "code": "NOT_FOUND", … }
GET  /papers/P                       Authorization: saA     → 200 { id: P, store_id: A, … }
GET  /papers/P                       Authorization: admin   → 200 { id: P, store_id: A, … }
GET  /papers/P                       Authorization: auditor → 200 { id: P, store_id: A, … }   (role is non-scoping)
POST /papers                         Authorization: auditor → 403   (unchanged — F-01 policy preserved)
```

Additionally, a `store_admin` whose JWT carries a null `storeId` now gets a clean `403 "Store admin has no assigned store"` instead of leaking everything — asserted by the `getPapers` and `getPaper` unit tests.

## 3) Test cases added

### Unit (`unit_tests/assessments.spec.ts`, new `paper tenant isolation (F-P1)` block — 12 cases)
**`getPapers`:**
- `store_admin: forces filter by JWT store, ignoring query storeId` — caller passes `storeId=store-B` while the JWT says `store-A`; `paperRepo.find` is called with `where: { store_id: 'store-A' }`.
- `store_admin without a store assignment → ForbiddenException`
- `platform_admin: no scope filter when storeId is omitted`
- `platform_admin: honors optional storeId query param`
- `auditor: no scope filter (read-only role is unchanged)`
- `content_reviewer: no scope filter (existing behavior preserved)`

**`getPaper`:**
- `store_admin A: 404 when the paper belongs to store B`
- `store_admin A: 200 when the paper belongs to store A`
- `platform_admin: can read any paper regardless of store_id`
- `auditor: can read any paper (read-only role is unchanged)`
- `missing paper: 404 for every role (unchanged)`
- `store_admin without a store: 403 on every paper read`

### API (`API_tests/remediation.api.spec.ts`, new `F-P1: Assessments paper tenant isolation` block — 7 cases)
1. `store_admin B listing papers does NOT include store A's paper` — asserts the A paper is absent, the B paper is present, and every row returned has `store_id === storeB.id`.
2. `store_admin B cannot bypass scope via ?storeId=<A> query param` — JWT scope wins; A paper stays hidden.
3. `store_admin B GET /papers/:id for store A paper → 404` — out-of-scope looks identical to "not found"; response body carries no `store_id`.
4. `store_admin A can GET their own store's paper → 200` — own-store read still works.
5. `store_admin A listing papers only sees store A` — symmetry check from the A side.
6. `platform_admin can GET the same paper → 200 (unchanged)` — platform_admin behavior is preserved.
7. `auditor read-only policy is preserved — GET allowed, POST denied` — regression guard against weakening the F-01 role policy.

Setup (timestamped for determinism): two stores `RemA{U}` / `RemB{U}`, two store_admin users `remsaA{U}` / `remsaB{U}` with distinct `storeId` assignments, one approved objective question per store, one paper scoped to each store — all seeded with `${Date.now()}` so re-runs don't collide.

## 5) Confirmation — cross-store paper read is blocked with 404

**Confirmed.** Both test layers verify it end-to-end:

- **Unit (`getPaper` store_admin A: 404 when the paper belongs to store B`):**
  ```ts
  paperRepo.findOne.mockResolvedValue({ id: 'p1', store_id: 'store-B', name: 'foreign' });
  await expect(service.getPaper('p1', storeAdminA)).rejects.toThrow(NotFoundException);
  ```
- **API (`store_admin B GET /papers/:id for store A paper → 404`):**
  ```ts
  const res = await request(server)
    .get(`/papers/${paperInStoreA}`)
    .set('Authorization', `Bearer ${storeAdminBToken}`);
  expect(res.status).toBe(404);
  expect(res.body).not.toHaveProperty('store_id');
  ```

Both tests pass in every validation run above. The 404 (not 403) policy is deliberate — cross-tenant existence leaks are hidden by making "no such paper" and "paper in another store" indistinguishable, matching the questions module's established behavior.

## 6) Residual risk

**None.**

- RBAC matrix is strict: auditor is read-only (F-01 unchanged — verified by the regression-guard test `auditor read-only policy is preserved`), content_reviewer and platform_admin behavior is unchanged (verified by both unit and API tests).
- Tenant isolation is enforced at the service layer, so direct service callers (cron, other services) inherit the same guard.
- Query `storeId` is never trusted for `store_admin` — verified by `store_admin B cannot bypass scope via ?storeId=<A> query param`.
- SQL-injection hardening in the quality module is untouched (no code changes outside `assessments.controller.ts`, `assessments.service.ts`, and their tests).
- No modules outside the assessments stack were modified. `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0. All four required validation commands pass.
